title: marsdenia latifolia
intro: Marsdenia latifolia, also known as the "Sarangkot" plant, is a perennial climber native to the tropical forests of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and clusters of white, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, anti-bacterial, and anti-fungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Marsdenia latifolia include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Marsdenia latifolia can be prepared into herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
